name: Serrey, Cham
intel:
Summary:
Cham Serrey is the foundation of the modern ghost weapon and explosive smuggling network. Operating out of Cambodia—a country characterized by a central alluvial plain surrounded by dense, heavily forested mountains like the Cardamom range—Serrey utilized a legitimate flour distribution company (JJH2+X2R) pinpoint the Kaopisat Group's central compound as a front for (TKG). His network was responsible for the global distribution of firearms, hard plastic/fibre ghost weapons, and the highly volatile "Ni-Redundant" explosives.

May 29th (2031):
At 0300 AM, the UETF executed a coordinated raid on suspected TKG hideouts (JJH2+X2R) pinpoint the Kaopisat Group's central compound to arrest Serrey and secure evidence. While Serrey successfully evaded the strike teams, the operation resulted in a catastrophic loss of inventory for TKG, with massive caches of weaponized metal tubes found disguised within flour shipments. Despite his escape and the lack of concrete evidence to finalize his arrest warrant, the raid effectively dismantled his regional monopoly. He is known to be a high-ranking administrator on the digital "Silk Route" bidding network.

name: Gavalas, Adrian
intel:
^Physical Description^
Heavily built frame developed through decades of military breaching and assault operations. His overall posture is forward-leaning and guarded, typical of operators accustomed to carrying heavy tactical equipment for extended periods.

His facial structure shows visible asymmetry caused by congenital craniofacial abnormalities associated with Goldenhar Syndrome. The left side of his face displays reduced zygomatic structure and slight maxillary deformation despite surgical reconstruction during adolescence.

The left eye remains functional but sits slightly deeper and lower within the orbital cavity due to early structural damage. Surgical scarring is visible across the orbital ridge and cheekbone area.

His voice is deep but characterized by mild articulation irregularities, caused by jaw alignment issues and muscle asymmetry. Speech is understandable but sometimes slowed or slightly distorted under stress or rapid communication.

Due to his height, facial scarring, and habit of wearing a balaclava, Gavalas is widely described by colleagues as physically intimidating even when non-aggressive.

^Body Marks^

• Surgical scar across the left orbital ridge and cheekbone from craniofacial reconstruction (age 12).
• Residual scarring around the left eyelid from neonatal orbital stabilization surgery.
• Multiple minor scars on arms, shoulders, and torso from breaching operations and close-quarters combat.
• Heavy callus development on palms and fingers caused by long-term climbing and mechanical breaching tool use.

Medical imaging confirms structural irregularities in cranial bones associated with Oculo-Auriculo-Vertebral Spectrum (Goldenhar Syndrome).

^Biography^

Adrian Gavalas was born 13 May 1989 in Thessaloniki, Greece under severe medical complications. His mother died during childbirth following a traumatic delivery caused by fetal macrosomia and undiagnosed craniofacial abnormalities in the infant. His father was never identified, leaving Gavalas without family guardianship.

He entered the Greek foster care system during infancy and would spend his childhood in three separate foster homes. His visible facial abnormalities and speech difficulty led to persistent social isolation and frequent conflicts with other children. Records indicate multiple runaway incidents during adolescence, during which Gavalas often survived outdoors for several days before being returned to custody.

During these periods he developed a strong interest in biology and primatology, reading extensively whenever books were available. His academic performance was described as intellectually strong but socially withdrawn. Climbing trees and abandoned structures became a routine habit, both as a form of escape and physical challenge.

At age eighteen he enrolled in Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, pursuing a biology degree. While academically capable, Gavalas struggled with authority and attendance requirements. At age 21, during his second year of study, he voluntarily withdrew from the program.

Shortly afterward he entered EKAM, the Hellenic Police Special Suppressive Antiterrorist Unit, Greece’s elite counter-terrorism police force. Training evaluations highlighted his exceptional strength, endurance, and spatial awareness, though instructors frequently noted difficulty maintaining strict command discipline.

At age 24, Gavalas became widely known within the unit during a hostage crisis in the Athens metro system. Armed attackers had taken control of a maintenance tunnel beneath the station while holding civilians hostage. While the main team attempted to locate an alternate access point, Gavalas independently entered the tunnel through a collapsed service shaft and initiated a breach.

His actions disrupted the attackers’ defensive setup and allowed the remainder of the team to enter and secure the area. All hostages were recovered alive. Although the operation succeeded, a formal review concluded that he had violated direct operational orders, and the incident was recorded as a disciplinary infraction. Command staff ultimately chose not to pursue punishment due to the positive outcome.

Gavalas later transferred into the Hellenic Army Special Forces, where his experience and physical attributes made him well suited for breaching and assault roles. Fellow operators began referring to him as “O Píthikos” (The Monkey) because of his climbing ability and unusual movement patterns during urban operations. The nickname eventually evolved into the callsign “Pithok.”

During the 2027 regional conflict linked to the Iran-Iraq war escalation, Greek forces participated in NATO operations across unstable areas of Anatolia. Gavalas served in multiple urban combat engagements where he developed a reputation for aggressive frontline tactics and unusually high endurance during sustained firefights.

Operational reports frequently reference his preferred weapon platform: an AK-103 rifle, chosen for reliability in debris-filled urban environments.

By his late thirties he had become known not only as a breacher but also as an effective communications coordinator and mission planner during complex operations. His ability to rapidly interpret battlefield conditions and coordinate movement under pressure resulted in his nomination for the UE-JTF (United Europe Joint Task Force).

At age 42, Adrian Gavalas was formally integrated into the UE-JTF multinational operator group.

On 13 November 2031, during a UE-JTF operation targeting a suspected bomb manufacturing facility in Rozhia, Gavalas and three other operators deviated from the authorized mission plan after extraction orders were issued. The facility was destroyed shortly afterward in a large explosion.

One operator later attempted to return to NATO territory and was arrested.

The remaining three operators — including Adrian Gavalas — disappeared following the incident.

^Last Seen^

|Date|: 13 November 2031

|Location|: Industrial zone near ||REDACTED||, Rozhia

Gavalas was last confirmed present with three UE-JTF operators during the unauthorized assault on a suspected explosives manufacturing site.

The facility was destroyed in a large detonation shortly after the assault began.

Following the event, Gavalas and two additional operators disappeared from all NATO tracking networks.

Current official status: MISSING IN ACTION / POSSIBLE ROGUE OPERATIVE

^Medical Evaluation^

Diagnosis: Goldenhar Syndrome (Oculo-Auriculo-Vertebral Spectrum)

Documented conditions include:

• Craniofacial asymmetry
• Reconstructed orbital structure (left side)
• Mandibular and maxillary irregularities

Despite visible deformities, neurological and visual function remain intact. Vision in both eyes is operational, though the left eye exhibits mild positional irregularity.

Speech evaluation confirms mild articulation impairment due to jaw alignment issues and facial muscle imbalance.

Military medical boards consistently classified him as Combat Fit, citing exceptional cardiovascular endurance and abnormal tolerance to pain and fatigue.

^Psychological Profile^

Psychological reviews indicate long-term effects of early childhood abandonment and social isolation. These factors contributed to elevated aggression responses and a preference for direct confrontation when resolving threats.

However, intelligence assessments consistently rate Gavalas as highly analytical, particularly in spatial reasoning and environmental interpretation during tactical operations.

He demonstrates strong loyalty toward team members and often prioritizes mission completion above personal safety. Command psychologists noted a persistent pattern of risk acceptance approaching self-sacrificial behavior.

In several evaluations, analysts suggested this behavior may stem from deeply ingrained identity issues connected to his early life experiences.

^Other Intel^

Known Callsign: Pithok

Originates from the Greek word “Píthikos” — Monkey.

|Operational Specialties|
• Breaching and forced entry
• Urban combat navigation
• Tactical communications coordination

Preferred weapon platform: AK-103 rifle

Following the Rozhia incident (2031), NATO intelligence files categorize Gavalas and the two remaining missing operators as:

<red>POTENTIAL ROGUE OPERATORS — LOCATION UNKNOWN<red>

Recovery or containment orders remain active under UE-JTF command authority.
